title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Execution Trace: Function Policy Centralization
summary: Authenticated users can enumerate another tenant’s records, mass-assign privileged fields, call admin actions through hidden routes, and infer denied objects from response differences. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo function policy centralization increment in deny-by-default authorization decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enforce function-level policy at a shared application boundary while retaining resource-specific checks and testing every route alias.
Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Function Policy Centralization · Theory Function Policy Centralization
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1220 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce tenant, action, and property authorization as resource policy; then drain the old revision while a canary fails its changed-case invariant. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce function-level policy at a shared application boundary while retaining resource-specific checks and testing every route alias.
